title: Kodikkunnu Bhagavathy Temple
tagline: Kodikkunnu Bhagavathy Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: The Kodikkunnu Bhagavathy Temple or Kodikkunnu Ambalam is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to the goddess Bhadrakali located in the village of Pallippuram, near Pattambi, in Palak
overview.description: The Kodikkunnu Bhagavathy Temple or Kodikkunnu Ambalam is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to the goddess Bhadrakali located in the village of Pallippuram, near Pattambi, in Palakkad district of Kerala, India.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Pallippuram, Palakkad
topPlaces.description: Pallippuram () is a village near Pattambi, Palakkad district, Kerala, India. Situated in the western extremity ('the western horn') of Palakkad district, part of the greater Paruthur Gramapanchayat, this place stands near the confluence of Bharathapuza River (Nila) and Tutha River (Tuthapuzha), and is bounded by these rivers to the south and west respectively. .

topPlaces.name: Parudur
topPlaces.description: Parudur is a village and gram panchayat in Pattambi taluk, Palakkad district in the state of Kerala, India. Parudur was originally a part of Pattambi Block Punjayath in Pattambi Taluk of erstwhile Palakkad district, before the formation of Malappuram district.

topPlaces.name: Trithala Maha Siva Temple
topPlaces.description: Trithala Maha Siva Temple (Malayalam: തൃത്താല മഹാ ശിവക്ഷേത്രം) is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva is situated on the banks of the Bharathappuzha river at Thrithala of Palakkad District in Kerala state in India. References to this temple are found in Aithihyamala of Kottarathil Sankunni and many classics of Malayalam Literature.

topPlaces.name: Thrithala
topPlaces.description: Thrithala is a town and a village in Pattambi taluk in Palakkad District of Kerala state, South India. The town is located along the banks of Bharathapuzha and is famed for its Shiva temple.

topPlaces.name: Pattithara
topPlaces.description: Pattithara is a village in Palakkad district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Kudallur
topPlaces.description: Kudallur is in the Palakkad district of Kerala. It is in Pattambi taluk, bordering Malappuram district, also on the banks of Bharathapuzha.

topPlaces.name: Kodumunda
topPlaces.description: Kodumunda is a small village in Muthuthala and Parudur gramapanchayath, Pattambi Taluk, Palakkad district Kerala. It is a minor trading center in Muthuthala Panchayath.

topPlaces.name: Malamakkavu Ayyappa Temple
topPlaces.description: Malamakkavu Ayyappa Temple is a famous Hindu temple dedicated to lord Ayyappan, located in Anakkara Panchayath in Palakkad district of Kerala. A special flower called "Chengazhinir Poovu" which is traditionally used as offering to deity is found and grown only in the temple pond.

items.note: Pallippuram () is a village near Pattambi, Palakkad district, Kerala, India. Situated in the western extremity ('the western horn') of Palakkad district, part of the greater Paruthur Gramapanchayat, this place stands near the confluence of Bharathapuza River (Nila) and Tutha River (Tuthapuzha), and is bounded by these rivers to the south and west respectively. .

Parudur is a village and gram panchayat in Pattambi taluk, Palakkad district in the state of Kerala, India. Parudur was originally a part of Pattambi Block Punjayath in Pattambi Taluk of erstwhile Palakkad district, before the formation of Malappuram district.

Trithala Maha Siva Temple (Malayalam: തൃത്താല മഹാ ശിവക്ഷേത്രം) is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva is situated on the banks of the Bharathappuzha river at Thrithala of Palakkad District in Kerala state in India. References to this temple are found in Aithihyamala of Kottarathil Sankunni and many classics of Malayalam Literature.
Thrithala is a town and a village in Pattambi taluk in Palakkad District of Kerala state, South India. The town is located along the banks of Bharathapuzha and is famed for its Shiva temple.
Pattithara is a village in Palakkad district in the state of Kerala, India.
Kudallur is in the Palakkad district of Kerala. It is in Pattambi taluk, bordering Malappuram district, also on the banks of Bharathapuzha.
Kodumunda is a small village in Muthuthala and Parudur gramapanchayath, Pattambi Taluk, Palakkad district Kerala. It is a minor trading center in Muthuthala Panchayath.

Malamakkavu Ayyappa Temple is a famous Hindu temple dedicated to lord Ayyappan, located in Anakkara Panchayath in Palakkad district of Kerala. A special flower called "Chengazhinir Poovu" which is traditionally used as offering to deity is found and grown only in the temple pond.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kodikkunnu Bhagavathy Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kodikkunnu Bhagavathy Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kodikkunnu Bhagavathy Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (38 km) and the nearest railway station is Coimbatore Junction (95 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kodikkunnu Bhagavathy Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kodikkunnu Bhagavathy Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kodikkunnu Bhagavathy Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kodikkunnu Bhagavathy Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kodikkunnu Bhagavathy Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Pallippuram, Palakkad, Parudur, Trithala Maha Siva Temple, Thrithala, Pattithara.
